trigger: Claude session shows 'Please run /login' / 'API Error: 403' / expired-token
rule: DO NOT send '/login' as a generic unstick. Mukul uses Bedrock (CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1, AWS_REGION=us-west-2) — '/login' is for Anthropic OAuth, not AWS STS. The correct unstick depends on his auth flow, which is project-specific. As of 2026-05-22 his ~/.zprofile has a 'claude-backend bedrock|sub' helper, but it likely just toggles env, not refreshes credentials. Before acting on a token-expiry symptom: (1) read ~/.zprofile / aws config to see what's actually used, (2) check awsCredentialExport / awsAuthRefresh in settings.json, (3) if no clear automation exists, SURFACE the stuck workspaces as a T3 needs_you proposal with the count + ws_refs + the suggested command — don't auto-execute. Token refresh that requires interactive SSO/MFA is never auto-fireable.
why: On 2026-05-22 the dispatcher saw 4 sessions stuck on 'Please run /login · API Error: 403' and unilaterally sent '/login' to 3 of them (workspace:98, :117, :122) without checking that they were on Bedrock. Mukul: 'I'll fix them... its fine. This is just really poor design. I don't have an assistant, I have a baby I need to babysit.' The right answer was T3 surface-with-context: tell him 'these 3 workspaces have expired Bedrock tokens, suggest you run X' — not chain-send a wrong command. Generic recovery actions on auth flows = babysit-bait.
